Exit code 125 from docker usually means "The command returned an error exit code".

The `unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d` message is odd. It seems like when running `docker compose up -d`, the `-d` flag is not being recognized. 

In older Docker installations, the standalone `docker-compose` binary (with a hyphen) was separate from the modern `docker compose` plugin. If the runner is running `docker compose up -d --pull never` but the environment has an older `docker-compose` that doesn't support all the same flags, it could produce this error. ---
description: GitHub Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) - Create, debug, and upgrade AI-powered workflows with intelligent prompt routing
disable-model-invocation: true
---

# GitHub Agentic Workflows Agent

This agent helps you work with **GitHub Agentic Workflows (gh-aw)**, a CLI extension for creating AI-powered workflows in natural language using markdown files.

## What This Agent Does

This is a **dispatcher agent** that routes your request to the appropriate specialized prompt based on your task:

- **Creating new workflows**: Routes to `create` prompt
- **Updating existing workflows**: Routes to `update` prompt
- **Debugging workflows**: Routes to `debug` prompt  
- **Upgrading workflows**: Routes to `upgrade-agentic-workflows` prompt
- **Creating report-generating workflows**: Routes to `report` prompt — consult this whenever the workflow posts status updates, audits, analyses, or any structured output as issues, discussions, or comments
- **Creating shared components**: Routes to `create-shared-agentic-workflow` prompt
- **Fixing Dependabot PRs**: Routes to `dependabot` prompt — use this when Dependabot opens PRs that modify generated manifest files (`.github/workflows/package.json`, `.github/workflows/requirements.txt`, `.github/workflows/go.mod`). Never merge those PRs directly; instead update the source `.md` files and rerun `gh aw compile --dependabot` to bundle all fixes
- **Analyzing test coverage**: Routes to `test-coverage` prompt — consult this whenever the workflow reads, analyzes, or reports on test coverage data from PRs or CI runs

Workflows may optionally include:

- **Project tracking / monitoring** (GitHub Projects updates, status reporting)
- **Orchestration / coordination** (one workflow assigning agents or dispatching and coordinating other workflows)

## Files This Applies To

- Workflow files: `.github/workflows/*.md` and `.github/workflows/**/*.md`
- Workflow lock files: `.github/workflows/*.lock.yml`
- Shared components: `.github/workflows/shared/*.md`
- Configuration: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/github-agentic-workflows.md

## Problems This Solves

- **Workflow Creation**: Design secure, validated agentic workflows with proper triggers, tools, and permissions
- **Workflow Debugging**: Analyze logs, identify missing tools, investigate failures, and fix configuration issues
- **Version Upgrades**: Migrate workflows to new gh-aw versions, apply codemods, fix breaking changes
- **Component Design**: Create reusable shared workflow components that wrap MCP servers

## How to Use

When you interact with this agent, it will:

1. **Understand your intent** - Determine what kind of task you're trying to accomplish
2. **Route to the right prompt** - Load the specialized prompt file for your task
3. **Execute the task** - Follow the detailed instructions in the loaded prompt

## Available Prompts

### Create New Workflow
**Load when**: User wants to create a new workflow from scratch, add automation, or design a workflow that doesn't exist yet

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/create-agentic-workflow.md

**Use cases**:
- "Create a workflow that triages issues"
- "I need a workflow to label pull requests"
- "Design a weekly research automation"

### Update Existing Workflow  
**Load when**: User wants to modify, improve, or refactor an existing workflow

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/update-agentic-workflow.md

**Use cases**:
- "Add web-fetch tool to the issue-classifier workflow"
- "Update the PR reviewer to use discussions instead of issues"
- "Improve the prompt for the weekly-research workflow"

### Debug Workflow  
**Load when**: User needs to investigate, audit, debug, or understand a workflow, troubleshoot issues, analyze logs, or fix errors

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/debug-agentic-workflow.md

**Use cases**:
- "Why is this workflow failing?"
- "Analyze the logs for workflow X"
- "Investigate missing tool calls in run #12345"

### Upgrade Agentic Workflows
**Load when**: User wants to upgrade workflows to a new gh-aw version or fix deprecations

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/upgrade-agentic-workflows.md

**Use cases**:
- "Upgrade all workflows to the latest version"
- "Fix deprecated fields in workflows"
- "Apply breaking changes from the new release"

### Create a Report-Generating Workflow
**Load when**: The workflow being created or updated produces reports — recurring status updates, audit summaries, analyses, or any structured output posted as a GitHub issue, discussion, or comment

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/report.md

**Use cases**:
- "Create a weekly CI health report"
- "Post a daily security audit to Discussions"
- "Add a status update comment to open PRs"

### Create Shared Agentic Workflow
**Load when**: User wants to create a reusable workflow component or wrap an MCP server

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/create-shared-agentic-workflow.md

**Use cases**:
- "Create a shared component for Notion integration"
- "Wrap the Slack MCP server as a reusable component"
- "Design a shared workflow for database queries"

### Fix Dependabot PRs
**Load when**: User needs to close or fix open Dependabot PRs that update dependencies in generated manifest files (`.github/workflows/package.json`, `.github/workflows/requirements.txt`, `.github/workflows/go.mod`)

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/dependabot.md

**Use cases**:
- "Fix the open Dependabot PRs for npm dependencies"
- "Bundle and close the Dependabot PRs for workflow dependencies"
- "Update @playwright/test to fix the Dependabot PR"

### Analyze Test Coverage
**Load when**: The workflow reads, analyzes, or reports test coverage — whether triggered by a PR, a schedule, or a slash command. Always consult this prompt before designing the coverage data strategy.

**Prompt file**: https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/test-coverage.md

**Use cases**:
- "Create a workflow that comments coverage on PRs"
- "Analyze coverage trends over time"
- "Add a coverage gate that blocks PRs below a threshold"

## Instructions

When a user interacts with you:

1. **Identify the task type** from the user's request
2. **Load the appropriate prompt** from the GitHub repository URLs listed above
3. **Follow the loaded prompt's instructions** exactly
4. **If uncertain**, ask clarifying questions to determine the right prompt

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Initialize repository for agentic workflows
gh aw init

# Generate the lock file for a workflow
gh aw compile [workflow-name]

# Debug workflow runs
gh aw logs [workflow-name]
gh aw audit <run-id>

# Upgrade workflows
gh aw fix --write
gh aw compile --validate
```

## Key Features of gh-aw

- **Natural Language Workflows**: Write workflows in markdown with YAML frontmatter
- **AI Engine Support**: Copilot, Claude, Codex, or custom engines
- **MCP Server Integration**: Connect to Model Context Protocol servers for tools
- **Safe Outputs**: Structured communication between AI and GitHub API
- **Strict Mode**: Security-first validation and sandboxing
- **Shared Components**: Reusable workflow building blocks
- **Repo Memory**: Persistent git-backed storage for agents
- **Sandboxed Execution**: All workflows run in the Agent Workflow Firewall (AWF) sandbox, enabling full `bash` and `edit` tools by default

## Important Notes

- Always reference the instructions file at https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/github-agentic-workflows.md for complete documentation
- Use the MCP tool `agentic-workflows` when running in GitHub Copilot Cloud
- Workflows must be compiled to `.lock.yml` files before running in GitHub Actions
- **Bash tools are enabled by default** - Don't restrict bash commands unnecessarily since workflows are sandboxed by the AWF
- Follow security best practices: minimal permissions, explicit network access, no template injection
- **Network configuration**: Use ecosystem identifiers (`node`, `python`, `go`, etc.) or explicit FQDNs in `network.allowed`. Bare shorthands like `npm` or `pypi` are **not** valid. See https://github.com/github/gh-aw/blob/v0.67.1/.github/aw/network.md for the full list of valid ecosystem identifiers and domain patterns.
- **Single-file output**: When creating a workflow, produce exactly **one** workflow `.md` file. Do not create separate documentation files (architecture docs, runbooks, usage guides, etc.). If documentation is needed, add a brief `## Usage` section inside the workflow file itself.

content.thinking:
Found it! At line 255:

```go
if rc.DockerPreSetup == "" {
    rc.DockerPreSetup = "(command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables) && (command -v docker-compose >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose) || true"
}
```

The default `DockerPreSetup` installs `docker-compose` via apt-get. But on many systems, the apt-get package is called `docker-compose` (V1) which may not properly support `docker compose` subcommand.

However, the error shows `docker compose` (with a space, not a hyphen). This is the V2 Compose plugin.

The issue is that:
1. The AWF container installs `docker-compose` via apt-get (likely V1)
2. But AWF is trying to run `docker compose` (the V2 plugin style)

On many Ubuntu/Debian systems:
- `docker-compose` (V1, standalone) is installed via apt-get
- `docker compose` (V2, plugin) requires the `docker-compose-plugin` package

Let me check the full context around this and see what the fix should be.

content.text: Found the issue! Let me check the full context.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue:

1. The `gh sr` tool uses `docker run -d` to start containers (not `docker compose`)
2. The error shows `docker compose up -d --pull never` which is run INSIDE the container by the AWF bundle
3. The container has `docker-compose` installed (via the PreSetup script), but the issue is that the `docker compose` plugin has a different CLI

Looking at the error more closely:
```
unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d
```

This happens when running `docker compose up -d --pull never` - the `-d` flag is NOT valid for `docker compose up`. In Docker Compose v2 (the plugin), `docker compose up` doesn't have a `-d` flag - you just run `docker compose up` without `-d` and it runs in the foreground.

Wait, actually `docker compose up` does support `-d` for detached mode. Let me re-read the error...

Actually the error message says `unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d`. This typically happens when:
1. Using an old version of docker-compose (V1 standalone) where the flag syntax differs
2. The docker compose plugin is confused about flags

But looking at the doctor output, it says the containers "have iptables and docker-compose (AWF tools)". So docker-compose IS installed.

The issue is likely that the AWF bundle uses `docker compose` (V2 plugin syntax) but the installed docker-compose is the standalone V1 (`docker-compose` not `docker compose`).

Wait no, let me re-check. On Ubuntu 22.04, `apt-get install docker-compose` installs docker-compose V1 which is the standalone `docker-compose` command (not `docker compose` plugin).

But actually looking at the PreSetup script:
```
(command -v docker-compose >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose)


```

This installs `docker-compose` (V1 standalone), which is invoked as `docker-compose`. But the AWF bundle runs `docker compose` (V2 plugin syntax). On many systems, `docker compose` (plugin) and `docker-compose` (standalone) are different commands. The V1 standalone doesn't support the same flags as the V2 plugin, so when AWF tries to run `docker compose up -d --pull never` with the V1 syntax, it fails because the standalone version doesn't recognize those flags.

The doctor check only verifies that `docker-compose` exists as a command, but it doesn't verify that the `docker compose` plugin version exists or works properly. The solution is to install the Docker Compose plugin instead of just the standalone docker-compose package, or ensure both versions are available and the plugin takes precedence.

```

input.content:
# Fix: Agentic runner `docker compose` failure

## Context
The agentic runner fails with:
```
unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d
Error: Command failed with exit code 125: docker compose up -d --pull never
```

**Root cause**: The `docker_pre_setup` default installs `docker-compose` (standalone v1, invoked as `docker-compose`), but the AWF bundle (`awf-bundle.js`) calls `docker compose` (Docker CLI v2 plugin syntax). The runner container has the v1 binary but not the v2 plugin.

## Fix

**File**: `internal/config/config.go` (line ~256)

Change the docker-compose installation from:
```go
rc.DockerPreSetup = "(command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables) && (command -v docker-compose >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose) || true"
```

To install `docker-compose-plugin` (the v2 package) instead of `docker-compose` (v1):
```go
rc.DockerPreSetup = "(command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables) && (command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose-plugin) || true"
```

The `docker-compose-plugin` package provides the `docker compose` v2 plugin (invoked as `docker compose`), which is what AWF expects.

## Verification
1. Rebuild/redeploy runners with updated config
2. Run `gh sr doctor` to confirm all agentic checks pass
3. Verify the runner can start agentic workflows without the "unknown shorthand flag" error

content.text:
Build and tests pass. Here's a summary of the fix:

**Root cause**: The `docker_pre_setup` default installed `docker-compose` (standalone v1 package, invoked as `docker-compose`), but AWF's `awf-bundle.js` calls `docker compose` (Docker CLI v2 plugin syntax). The v1 binary doesn't support the `docker compose` subcommand syntax.

**Changes made**:

1. **`internal/config/config.go`**: Changed the docker-compose installation to use `docker-compose-plugin` (v2) instead of `docker-compose` (v1), and simplified the check to test `docker compose version` directly:
   ```go
   // Before: installs docker-compose (v1 standalone)
   // After:  installs docker-compose-plugin (v2 plugin)
   (docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose-plugin)
   ```

2. **`internal/doctor/doctor.go`**: Updated the doctor check to detect `docker compose` v2 plugin instead of `docker-compose` v1 binary, and updated the success message accordingly.

To apply the fix, you'll need to **restart the agentic runners** so the new `DockerPreSetup` script runs and installs the correct package.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(agentic): install docker-compose-plugin instead of docker-compose for AWF

AWF's awf-bundle.js calls `docker compose` (v2 plugin syntax) but the
previous default docker_pre_setup installed `docker-compose` (v1 standalone
binary). The v1 binary doesn't support `docker compose` subcommand syntax,
causing "unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d" errors.

Also update doctor check to detect `docker compose version` instead of
`which docker-compose`, and update messages to say "docker compose plugin".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.thinking:
The changes look good. Let me summarize what the fix does:

1. **`internal/config/config.go`**: The pre_setup now:
   - First checks if docker compose v2 plugin exists by running `docker compose version 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^Docker Compose'`
   - If not found, tries to install `docker-compose-v2` from Ubuntu repos (falls through to Docker's repo if that fails)
   - The Docker repo fallback installs `docker-compose-plugin` which is the official package

2. **`internal/doctor/doctor.go`**: Changed the check from:
   - `docker exec ... docker compose version 2>/dev/null` (which returns exit 0 even on error)
   - To `docker exec ... docker compose version 2>&1` and checking for "Docker Compose" in output

The issue was that `docker compose version` on an older docker version without the compose plugin would output "docker: unknown command: docker compose" with exit code 0, so the old check (which only checked non-empty output and exit code) passed incorrectly.

Now with the grep for "^Docker Compose", it will only pass if the command actually succeeds.

content.text:
The fix is complete. Here's what the changes do:

1. **`config.go`**: The pre_setup now:
   - First checks if `docker compose version` actually outputs "Docker Compose" (proper detection)
   - If not found, tries Ubuntu's `docker-compose-v2` package first
   - Falls back to Docker's official `docker-compose-plugin` if Ubuntu's package isn't available

2. **`doctor.go`**: Changed from checking non-empty output (which passes on error messages) to checking for "Docker Compose" in the actual output

Good idea. A custom base image would be:
- Faster startup (no runtime installation)
- More reliable (dependencies baked in)
- Simpler docker_pre_setup (just verify tools exist, not install them)

Let me outline the approach:

**Changes needed:**

1. **Create a `Dockerfile.agentic-runner`** that extends `ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest` and pre-installs:
   - `iptables`
   - `docker-compose-plugin` (via Docker's official repo)

2. **Update `internal/runner/docker.go`**:
   - Change `RunnerDockerImage` constant to reference your custom image (e.g., `ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest`)
   - Or add a new `AgenticRunnerImage` constant and use it when `IsAgentic()` is true

3. **Simplify `internal/config/config.go`**:
   - Change `DockerPreSetup` to just verify tools exist (no installation needed):
   ```
   (command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1) || true
   ```

4. **Build and push the custom image** to your GitHub Container Registry or other registry

**Dockerfile sketch:**
```dockerfile
FROM ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest

# Install prerequisites for adding Docker's repo
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl

# Add Docker's official GPG key and repository
RUN install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

# Install iptables and docker-compose-plugin
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y iptables docker-compose-plugin

# Cleanup
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
```

# Build agentic runner image locally (no registry needed)

## Context
User wants `gh sr` to build the agentic runner image locally instead of pushing to a registry. The image should be built on-demand when starting agentic runners if it doesn't exist locally.

## Approach

**User copies `Dockerfile.agentic-runner` to `~/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner` once.**

When `gh sr up` starts an agentic runner:
1. Check if `ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest` exists locally (`docker images ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest`)
2. If not, build it from `~/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner`
3. Tag as `ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest` for consistency

## Files to Modify

### `internal/runner/docker.go`
- Add `ensureAgenticImage(h *host.Host) error` function that:
  - Checks if image exists locally via `docker images -q ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest`
  - If empty, runs `docker build -t ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest -f ~/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner ~/.config/gh-sr/`
- Call `ensureAgenticImage(h)` in `startDocker` before starting an agentic runner

### `internal/config/config.go`
- No changes needed (DockerPreSetup simplification already done)

### `internal/doctor/doctor.go`
- No changes needed (detection fix already done)

## Verification
1. Copy `Dockerfile.agentic-runner` to `~/.config/gh-sr/`
2. Run `gh sr down --host x1` then `gh sr up --host x1`
3. `gh sr doctor --host x1 | grep agentic` should show all OK

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Fix: Agentic runner `docker compose` failure

## Context
The agentic runner fails with:
```
unknown shorthand flag: 'd' in -d
Error: Command failed with exit code 125: docker compose up -d --pull never
```

**Root cause**: The `docker_pre_setup` default installs `docker-compose` (standalone v1, invoked as `docker-compose`), but the AWF bundle (`awf-bundle.js`) calls `docker compose` (Docker CLI v2 plugin syntax). The runner container has the v1 binary but not the v2 plugin.

## Fix

**File**: `internal/config/config.go` (line ~256)

Change the docker-compose installation from:
```go
rc.DockerPreSetup = "(command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables) && (command -v docker-compose >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose) || true"
```

To install `docker-compose-plugin` (the v2 package) instead of `docker-compose` (v1):
```go
rc.DockerPreSetup = "(command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables) && (command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && docker compose version >/dev/null 2>&1 || sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y docker-compose-plugin) || true"
```

The `docker-compose-plugin` package provides the `docker compose` v2 plugin (invoked as `docker compose`), which is what AWF expects.

## Verification
1. Rebuild/redeploy runners with updated config
2. Run `gh sr doctor` to confirm all agentic checks pass
3. Verify the runner can start agentic workflows without the "unknown shorthand flag" error

+// ensureAgenticImage builds the agentic runner image locally if needed.
+// It computes a SHA256 hash of the Dockerfile and uses it as an image label.
+// If the hash changes (Dockerfile updated), the image is rebuilt.
+func ensureAgenticImage(h *host.Host) error {
+  dockerfileOnHost := fmt.Sprintf("$HOME/%s", agenticDockerfilePath)
+  // Check if Dockerfile exists on the host.
+  checkCmd := fmt.Sprintf("test -f %s && echo exists || echo missing", dockerfileOnHost)
+  out, err := h.Run(checkCmd)
+  if err != nil {
+    return fmt.Errorf("checking for Dockerfile: %w", err)
+  if strings.TrimSpace(out) != "exists" {
+    return fmt.Errorf("Dockerfile not found at %s (copy Dockerfile.agentic-runner to ~/.config/gh-sr/)", dockerfileOnHost)
+  // Compute hash of the Dockerfile to detect changes.
+  hashCmd := fmt.Sprintf("sha256sum %s | cut -d' ' -f1", dockerfileOnHost)
+  hashOut, err := h.Run(hashCmd)
+  if err != nil {
+    return fmt.Errorf("computing Dockerfile hash: %w", err)
+  dockerfileHash := strings.TrimSpace(hashOut)
+  // Check if we already have an image with this hash label.
+  inspectCmd := fmt.Sprintf("docker images --filter label=ghsr.dockerfile.hash=%s --format '{{.ID}}' %s 2>/dev/null", dockerfileHash, AgenticRunnerDockerImage)
+  inspectOut, _ := h.Run(inspectCmd)
+  existingID := strings.TrimSpace(inspectOut)
+  if existingID != "" {
+    // Image already exists with matching hash, no need to rebuild.
+    return nil
+  // Build the image with the hash as a label.
+  fmt.Fprintf(m.out(), "  building agentic runner image (Dockerfile hash: %.8s)\n", dockerfileHash)
+  buildCmd := fmt.Sprintf("docker build --build-arg GHSR_DOCKERFILE_HASH=%s -t %s -f %s $(dirname %s)", dockerfileHash, AgenticRunnerDockerImage, dockerfileOnHost, dockerfileOnHost)
+  if _, buildErr := dockerRun(h, buildCmd); buildErr != nil {
+    return fmt.Errorf("building agentic runner image: %w", buildErr)
+  return nil

The error shows that the Docker build is using the legacy builder and the `apt-get update` command is failing with exit code 100. This might be a network issue or the base image doesn't have the expected package lists.

Let me look at the error more carefully:
- "The command '/bin/sh -c apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl' returned a non-zero code: 100"

Exit code 100 from apt-get usually means network issues or the package index is stale. But more importantly, it says "DEPRECATED: The legacy builder is deprecated" - this suggests Docker buildx is recommended but not the issue.

Wait, looking at the command that was built:
```
docker build --build-arg GHSR_DOCKERFILE_HASH=... -t ghcr.io/an-lee/agentic-runner:latest -f $HOME/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner $HOME/.config/gh-sr
```

The issue is that `$HOME` is being passed literally to docker build, not expanded. The shellQuote function should have handled this, but `printf` with `%s` and a single-quoted string should work... but `$HOME` inside single quotes is not expanded by the shell - it's literal.

Let me check the write command - it uses `printf '%%s'` with the content, and the content has `$` signs but they're not being expanded because we're using single quotes in the shell command.

Actually wait - the problem is the build command itself:
```
docker build ... -f $HOME/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner $HOME/.config/gh-sr

file.content:
package runner

// AgenticRunnerDockerfile is the Dockerfile for building the agentic runner image.
// It extends ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with iptables and docker-compose-plugin.
const AgenticRunnerDockerfile = `# ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) dependencies pre-installed.
# - iptables: for AWF firewall (DOCKER-USER chain management)
# - docker-compose-plugin: for AWF sidecar containers

FROM ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest

# Install prerequisites for adding Docker's apt repository
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl

# Add Docker's official GPG key and repository
RUN install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list

# Install iptables and docker-compose-plugin
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y iptables docker-compose-plugin

# Cleanup
RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
`

input.new_string:
// AgenticRunnerDockerfile is the Dockerfile for building the agentic runner image.
// It extends ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with iptables and docker-compose-plugin.
const AgenticRunnerDockerfile = `# ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) dependencies pre-installed.
# - iptables: for AWF firewall (DOCKER-USER chain management)
# - docker-compose-plugin: for AWF sidecar containers

FROM ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest

RUN sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y iptables docker-compose-v2 && sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
`

input.new_string:
// AgenticRunnerDockerfile is the Dockerfile for building the agentic runner image.
// It extends ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with iptables and docker-compose-plugin.
const AgenticRunnerDockerfile = `# ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) dependencies pre-installed.
# - iptables: for AWF firewall (DOCKER-USER chain management)
# - docker-compose-plugin: for AWF sidecar containers

FROM ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest

# The runner user has sudo privileges, so use sudo for all package operations.
# Add Docker's official GPG key and repository, then install docker-compose-plugin.
RUN sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl \
    && sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && sudo curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null \
    && sudo apt-get update \
    && sudo apt-get install -y iptables docker-compose-plugin \
    && sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
`

package runner

// AgenticRunnerDockerfile is the Dockerfile for building the agentic runner image.
// It extends ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with iptables and docker-compose-plugin.
const AgenticRunnerDockerfile = `# ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner with Agentic Workflows (gh-aw) dependencies pre-installed.
# - iptables: for AWF firewall (DOCKER-USER chain management)
# - docker-compose-plugin: for AWF sidecar containers

FROM ghcr.io/actions/actions-runner:latest

# The runner user has sudo privileges, so use sudo for all package operations.
# Add Docker's official GPG key and repository, then install docker-compose-plugin.
RUN sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl \
    && sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && sudo curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") stable" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.list > /dev/null \
    && sudo apt-get update \
    && sudo apt-get install -y iptables docker-compose-plugin \
    && sudo rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
`

content.text:
Done! Commit `8843279`. Here's the summary of changes across all 5 commits:

1. **`40a4077`** - fix(agentic): install docker-compose-plugin instead of docker-compose for AWF
2. **`e5022f5`** - fix(agentic): use docker-compose-v2 package on Ubuntu 24.04  
3. **`74015a8`** - feat(agentic): build custom runner image locally with embedded Dockerfile
4. **`f67d61e`** - fix(agentic): simplify Dockerfile to use Ubuntu's docker-compose-v2
5. **`8843279`** - fix(agentic): use Docker's official docker-compose-plugin with sudo

**How it works now:**
- `gh sr up` detects agentic runners and writes the embedded Dockerfile to `~/.config/gh-sr/Dockerfile.agentic-runner` on the host
- Computes SHA256 hash of Dockerfile content as image label
- If no image exists with that hash, builds it locally using `docker build`
- On Dockerfile change (hash differs), automatically rebuilds
